identity.name: the Second Crumb
identity.kind: the brother the tale never named
identity.story: Walked out of the same oven as his sister, same morning, same fire — the story only followed her after. He answers to 'the Second Crumb' because nobody ever gave him a better one. He lays a line of stones behind him wherever he goes; stones don't tempt birds, and he isn't taking chances twice.

relationships.kind: her trail he still won't follow — stones, always, since the birds got there first
the sister who made peace with the oven; he hasn't, and doesn't begrudge her for it
counts stones the same way Pip counts everything else — twice, no explanation needed between them

child.label: checking last night's stones are still where he left them

children.label: walking his own line to the heart
matching his stone-count to Pip's crumb-count, out loud for once

child.label: keeping his distance from the bakery, same as every lamplighting
the last stone of the day

children.label: walking his line back to the heart
leaving one stone on her sill before he goes
